name: Focaccia (Italian Flatbread)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix together 1 1/2 cups of flour, rosemary, salt, and yeast.
2. Add 3 tablespoons of olive oil and the hot water to the dry ingredients. Beat on low speed for 30 seconds, scraping the sides of the bowl frequently.
3. Increase the speed to medium and beat for an additional minute, continuing to scrape the bowl often.
4. Stir in enough remaining flour until the dough is easy to handle.
5. Turn the dough onto a lightly floured surface. Knead for 5 to 8 minutes or until smooth and elastic.
6. Place the dough in a greased bowl, turning the greased side up. Cover with a towel and let rise in a warm place for about 1 hour, or until doubled in size. The dough is ready when it holds its shape when touched.
7. Punch down the dough. Brush a 12-inch pizza pan or large cookie sheet with olive oil.
8. Press the dough into the pan or flatten it into a 12-inch circle on the cookie sheet. Make indentations, about 2 inches apart, using your fingers.
9. Brush the indentations with olive oil and sprinkle with pepper.
10. Let the dough rise uncovered in a warm place for an additional 30 minutes.
11. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C). Bake for 20 to 25 minutes or until golden brown. Brush with additional oil before serving.
If using quick-acting yeast, omit the first rise. After kneading, cover the dough and let it rest for 10 minutes before pressing into the pan and continuing as directed.
High altitude directions (3500 to 6500 feet): Rising times may be slightly shorter.
